key_differences: Without skill: wrong OpenAPI version (3.0.0 vs 3.1.1), no paths files, empty schemas, missing cookie.json and header.json params, no README, no Default environment
With skill: complete structure, all conventions followed perfectly
Without skill: missing 'servers' field in standalone OpenAPI doc, camelCase operationIds (loginUser) instead of kebab-case (auth-login)
With skill: all conventions including servers:[], kebab-case operationIds, correct $ref format
Without skill: environments.json missing env_<name> ID pattern, missing createdAt/updatedAt timestamps, missing Default environment
With skill: proper IDs (env_default, env_staging), timestamps, both Default and Staging environments

analyst_notes: The skill shows strongest improvement on the scaffold task (36% → 100%), where the baseline missed most structural requirements
Convention adherence (operationId naming, standalone doc format, environment IDs) is consistently better with the skill
The baseline performed reasonably on auth endpoints (80%) since the prompt mentioned OpenAPI Puer conventions, but still missed server field and naming conventions
No assertions appear non-discriminating — all test areas show measurable differences between with/without skill
